title: The Wildest Sun A Novel
author: Asha Lemmie
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Wildest Sun A Novel" book by Asha Lemmie?
Options:
A. I had only met him four or five times before coming to stay here, on his infrequent trips back to Paris. I have always thought him very handsome—tall, bronze skinned, with wavy hair that he gels back, and flashing, mischievous dark eyes. He is from Louisiana originally, and speaks quite good French from his time in Paris in the twenties. His wife is fluent. Even though our shared language is the reason I chose to write to him weeks ago, to beg for refuge in America, I also happen to prefer him above the others in Maman’s remaining circle of confidants. If I had to grind my pride to dust and throw myself at the feet of any one of them, I am glad that it is him. 
B. I had only met him a few times before coming to stay here, during his occasional visits back to Paris. I've always considered him very good-looking—tall, tanned, with wavy hair that he slicks back, and lively, playful dark eyes. He's originally from Louisiana, and speaks quite decent French from his time in Paris in the 1920s. His wife is fluent. Even though our shared language is why I decided to write him weeks ago, pleading for refuge in America, I also just happen to like him best out of Maman's remaining close friends. If I had to completely humble myself and beg at the feet of any of them, I'm glad it's him.
C. I had only met with him four or five times before, during his sporadic visits back to Paris. I've always considered him very handsome—tall, tanned, with wavy hair that he slicks down, and lively, playful dark eyes. He's originally from Louisiana, and speaks decent French from his time spent in Paris during the 1920s. His wife is fluent too. Even though the fact we share a language was why I decided to write him weeks ago, begging for refuge in America, I also happen to like him most out of Maman's remaining inner circle. If I was made to swallow my pride and throw myself at the mercy of any of them, I'm glad it's him.
D. I had only encountered him four or five times previously, when he occasionally returned to Paris. I've always found him very attractive—tall, bronze-skinned, with wavy hair that he styles back, and bright, mischievous dark eyes. He's from Louisiana originally, and speaks quite good French from living in Paris in the 1920s. His wife is fluent as well. Although our common language was the reason I chose to contact him weeks ago, pleading for asylum in America, I also prefer him over Maman's other confidants. If I was forced to debase myself and grovel at the feet of any of them, I'm pleased it's him.  
Answer:

If I had to grind my pride to dust and throw myself at the feet of any one of them, I am glad that it is him.
